title: Polite Intonation & Formal Words
learningGoal: Master the pronunciation of polite request forms (Could you, Would you) and formal business vocabulary with correct stress and intonation.
instructions: ビジネス英語の発音練習です。「Could you」や「Would you」の音の繋がり（リンキング）と、フォーマルな単語のアクセント（強勢）に注意して発音してください。丁寧な印象を与えるため、少しゆっくりと落ち着いて話すのがポイントです。(Practice the pronunciation of polite requests and formal vocabulary. Pay attention to linking and word stress.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Link the 'd' and 'y' to make a 'j' sound. Coo-joo.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'Kudo yu' with a strong 'o' sound.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: ウジュー マインド
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Woo-joo mind. Keep the 'd' at the end of mind soft.
Stress the second syllable: a-POL-o-gize. The 'o' is short like 'ah'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: インコンヴィーニエンス

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 空いている、利用できる
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Stress the second syllable: a-VAIL-a-ble. Make sure to bite your bottom lip for the 'v'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 予定を立てる、スケジュール
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: In American English: SKED-jool. In British: SHED-yool. Choose one.
Stress the second syllable: con-FIRM. Don't pronounce the 'r' too hard.
Stress the second syllable: post-PONE. The 't' is often silent.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: ルック フォワード トゥ

expectedPronunciationText: Could you Would you Would you mind apologize inconvenience available schedule Sincerely appreciate confirm postpone look forward to

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! Your pronunciation is very professional. The linking in 'Could you' and 'Would you' sounds perfect.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Pay close attention to the stressed syllables in the long words. For example, a-POL-o-gize, not A-po-lo-gize.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Practice the 'v' sound in 'available' and 'inconvenience' by biting your bottom lip.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: Could you Would you Would you mind apologize inconvenience available schedule Sincerely appreciate confirm postpone look forward to
acceptedVariants: could you would you would you mind apologize inconvenience available schedule sincerely appreciate confirm postpone look forward to
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: Could you Would you Would you mind apologize inconvenience available schedule Sincerely appreciate confirm postpone look forward to
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: coojoo woojoo woojoomind ahpahlohgyze inconveenyents ahvayluhbul skehjool sinseerlee ahpreesheeyate confirm postpone lookforwerdtuh

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Could you, Would you, Would you mind, apologize, inconvenience, available, schedule, Sincerely, appreciate, confirm, postpone, look forward to

hintText: Long words in English have one main syllable that is stressed (louder and longer). Listen to the audio to find it!
